title: Australia World Cup Squad 2026: Full Socceroos Squad, Player Profiles and Team Analysis
original_text:
Australia’s FIFA World Cup 2026 squad in full, including every Socceroo selected, club teams, positions, caps, goals and tournament analysis.

Australia is ready for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Tony Popovic and his 26-man squad based in Oakland as they prepare for their Group D matches and hopefully beyond.

The Socceroos’ key stars for this tournament look set to be names like Nestory Irankunda, Mohamed Toure, Jordan Bos, Maty Ryan, Jackson Irvine, Alessandro Circati and Harry Souttar.

OUR COVERAGE: Football360’s unbeatable World Cup line-up ready to write Socceroos’ next chapter

In a fairly evenly matched group up against the likes of hosts the United States, Türkiye and Paraguay, the expectation will be that this side competes and goes through to the Round of 32.

Right attacking midfield: Mathew Leckie

Striker: Mohamed Toure

Australia’s Key Players at the FIFA World Cup

Nestory Irankunda

Explosive Watford winger Nestory Irankunda looms as the Socceroos’ x-factor in this World Cup. He’s lighting quick and has one of the most powerful strikes in the game. Watch out for him down Australia’s right, while he has also been trialled centrally as a striker in recent months. At just 20 years old, this could be a breakout tournament for the Adelaide United product.

Mathew Ryan

Socceroos captain Maty Ryan will be between the sticks at a record-equalling fourth World Cup; the only other Australians to play at football’s biggest stage that many times are Mathew Leckie, Mark Milligan and Tim Cahill. Ryan is a brick wall between the sticks and arrives at this tournament on the back of helping Levante retain their top-flight status in Spain. At 35 years old, he brings bags of experience and is still at the top of his game.

Jackson Irvine

St Pauli captain Irvine is heading to his third World Cup and remains a supremely important figure within Australia’s midfield. He helps to set the tempo in the centre of the park, but also to get the Socceroos up the field in Popovic’s system, where width is provided by wing-backs and the wide attackers are inverted. Irvine is very experienced and is a leader within this team.

Harry Souttar

Colossal defender Harry Souttar is a marvel, having now twice recovered from major injuries just in time to star for Australia at the World Cup. This time around, he has returned from 18 months out with an Achilles injury and looks set to be a crucial cog in the defence. Not only does he make important blocks at the back, his size is also a factor at the other end, where is a dangerous weapon from set pieces.

Jordan Bos

The Feyenoord defender is another candidate to be Australia’s breakout player of this tournament. He plays a key role as the attacking outlet from left wing-back, driving forward with creativity and speed. This system feels built for him. If he excels, he could well attract Premier League interest, having produced double figures in assists in The Netherlands this season.

Australia’s World Cup History

Appearances: 7

Best Finish: Round of 16 (2006, 2022)

Biggest win: Australia 3-1 Japan (Group Stage, 2006 FIFA World Cup in Germany)

Most appearances: Mathew Ryan and Mathew Leckie – 10 World Cup appearances each.

Most goals: Tim Cahill – 5 World Cup goals

FAQs

Who is Australia’s captain at the World Cup?

Mathew Ryan is the Socceroos’ captain at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Australia’s goalkeeper is heading into his record-equalling fourth World Cup on the back of a brilliant season in LaLiga with Levante.

How many players are in Australia’s World Cup squad?

There are 26 players in the squad, which is the maximum amount FIFA allows.

Who is Australia’s youngest player?

Defender Lucas Herrington is the side’s youngest player at just 18 years old. Should he start any of the three matches, he would become the youngest player to be included in a starting XI for the Socceroos in the World Cup, taking the record from Adam Taggart who was 21 when he started against Spain in 2014.

Which A-League players made the squad?

There are five players currently representing A-League clubs in this squad. Melbourne City have three players (Patrick Beach, Aziz Behich, Mathew Leckie), while Sydney FC (Paul Okon-Engstler) and Melbourne Victory (Nishan Velupillay) have one each.

Who is Australia’s most experienced player?

That would also be Mathew Ryan. The 35-year-old has 104 caps and is closing in on his idol Mark Schwarzer’s record, which stands at 109 caps.

Has Australia ever reached a World Cup quarter-final?

No. The Socceroos’ best result at the men’s World Cup is the Round of 16, which was achieved in both 2006 under Guus Hiddink and 2022 under Graham Arnold, who is coaching at this tournament with Iraq.
